Monday.name: Cross-Body Lat Pull-Around
Monday.notes: Try to keep the cable and your wrist aligned in a straight line throughout the pull. Feel a nice, deep lat stretch at the top.

Monday.name: Snatch-Grip RDL
Monday.notes: The RPE is intentionally low here because these will cause a lot of muscle damage. Don't be tempted to go too heavy. 1 second pause at the bottom of each rep. To keep tension on the hamstrings, stop about 75% of the way to full lockout on each rep (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the range of motion).

Monday.name: Chest-Supported Machine Row
Monday.notes: Flare elbows out at roughly 45° and squeeze your shoulder blades together hard at the top of each rep.

Monday.name: Straight-Bar Lat Prayer
Monday.notes: Lean forward to get a big stretch on the lats at the top of the ROM and then stand upright as you squeeze your lats at the bottom.

Monday.name: Hammer Preacher Curl
Monday.notes: These will target the brachialis and forearms hard. Squeeze the dumbbell hard in the middle of the handle and curl about 3/4 of the way up (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the curl).

Monday.name: Lying Paused Rope Face Pull
Monday.notes: Pause for 1-2 seconds in the squeeze of each rep. Contract the rear delts hard!

Tuesday.name: Cuffed Behind-The-Back Lateral Raise
Tuesday.notes: Raise the cables up and out in a "Y" motion. Really try to connect with the middle delt fibers as you sweep the weight up and out.

Tuesday.name: Low Incline Smith Machine Press
Tuesday.notes: Set the bench at a ~15° incline. 1 second pause on the chest on each rep while maintaining tension on the pecs.

Tuesday.name: Pec Deck (w/ Integrated Partials)
Tuesday.notes: Set up the pec deck to allow for maximum stretch. On all sets, alternate full-ROM reps and half-ROM reps (i.e. do 1 rep with full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM (in the stretched/bottom half), then 1 rep full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM). Repeat until you've reached the target reps and an RPE of 9-10.
substitutions: Bent-Over Cable Pec Flye (w/ Integrated Partials)

Tuesday.name: Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (Bar)
Tuesday.notes: Feel a nasty stretch on the triceps throughout the entire negative. Pause for 1 second in the stretch part of each rep.

Tuesday.name: Triceps Pressdown (Bar)
Tuesday.notes: These are meant to be fairly heavy, which is why we're using a bar instead of a rope. Aim to add some weight week to week at the set rep target. Always keep the form tight as you overload the triceps.

Tuesday.name: Cable Crunch
Tuesday.notes: Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.

Wednesday.name: Seated Leg Curl
Wednesday.notes: Lean forward over the machine to get a maximum stretch in your hamstrings.

Wednesday.name: Machine Hip Adduction
Wednesday.notes: Mind-muscle connection with your inner thighs. These are great for adding thigh mass from the front! Push them hard!

Wednesday.name: Hack Squat
Wednesday.notes: We're using a reverse pyramid on this exercise. Warm-up as usual to your first working set for 4 reps. This first set will be your heaviest set. Then for set 2, drop the weight back ~10-15% and do 6 reps. Then for set 3, drop the weight back another 10-15% and do 8 reps.

Wednesday.name: Leg Extension
Wednesday.notes: Set the seat back as far as it will go while still feeling comfortable. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat. Use a 2-3 second negative. Feel your quads pulling apart on the negative.

Wednesday.name: Leg Press Calf Press
Wednesday.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

Thursday.name: Weak Point Exercise 1
Thursday.notes: Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

Thursday.name: Weak Point Exercise 2 (optional)
Thursday.notes: If your weak point is feeling recovered (not sore or fatigued) then feel free to hit Exercise 2. If your weak point is feeling tired or sore, do not perform the second weak point exercise this week.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

Thursday.name: Bayesian Cable Curl
Thursday.notes: If you have a left-right bicep size imbalance, do these 1 arm at a time, starting with the weaker arm. Take the weaker arm to an RPE of 9-10. Then match the reps with the other arm (stop once you've matched the reps, even if the RPE is lower). If you don't have a size imbalance, do these both arms at the same time.

Thursday.name: Seated DB French Press
Thursday.notes: Place both palms under the head of a dumbbell and perform overhead extensions. Feel a deep stretch on your triceps at the bottom. Avoid pausing at the top of each rep.

Thursday.name: Bottom-2/3 Constant Tension Preacher Curl
Thursday.notes: Stay in the bottom 2/3 of the curl. Don't squeeze all the way up to the top. Keep your triceps firmly pinned against the pad as you curl. No pausing at the top or bottom: constant tension on the biceps!

Thursday.name: Cable Triceps Kickback
Thursday.notes: There are two ways you can do this: upright or bent over. Choose the one that feels more comfortable for you. The main thing is that when you're in the full squeeze, your shoulder should be positioned back behind your torso.

Thursday.name: Roman Chair Leg Raise
Thursday.notes: Allow your lower back to round as you curl your legs up. 10-20 reps is a broad range on purpose: just go until you hit RPE 9-10 (0-1 reps shy of failure) with controlled form.

Friday.name: Super-ROM Overhand Cable Row
Friday.notes: Set up a wide grip pulldown bar on a seated cable row. Using a double overhand grip, perform rows while leaning forward on the negative and then extend your torso to be upright as you finish the row.

Friday.name: Arms-Extended 45° Hyperextension
Friday.notes: Keep your arms extended out at 45° as you do back extensions. You should feel a crazy burn in your mid-back and lower back.

Friday.name: Lean-Back Lat Pulldown
Friday.notes: Initiate the pulldown with a straight up posture. As you pull the bar down, lean back by about 15-30° to get the mid-back more involved. Softly touch the bar to your chest on every rep and, even though you're leaning back, still control the weight!

Friday.name: Inverse DB Zottman Curl
Friday.notes: Do a hammer curl on the positive, then turn your palms facing up at the top and use a palms-up grip on the negative.

Friday.name: Cable Reverse Flye (Mechanical Dropset)
Friday.notes: You'll probably want to watch the video for this one. Take ~3 big steps back from the cable machine and do your first 5 reps. After those first 5 reps, immediately (without resting) take 1 step forward and do another 4 reps. Then (without resting) take another step forward and do at least another 3 reps (or until you hit RPE 9-10).

Friday.name: Cable Paused Shrug-In
Friday.notes: Shrug up and in. Think about shrugging "up to your ears". 1-2 second pause in the squeeze (at the top) of each rep, then another 1-2 second pause in the stretch (at the bottom) of each rep.

Saturday.name: Machine Shoulder Press
Saturday.notes: Ensure that your elbows break at least 90°. Mind-muscle connection with your delts. Smooth, controlled reps.

Saturday.name: Cross-Body Cable Y-Raise
Saturday.notes: Think about "drawing a sword" as you do the positive. Sweep your arm up, out and across your body. It may take a few weeks to get used to these if you haven't done them before, but once they click, they really click.

Saturday.name: Paused Assisted Dip
Saturday.notes: Slow 2-3 second negative. 1-2 second pause at the bottom. Explode with control on the way up. Go as deep as your shoulders comfortably allow, trying to at least break a 90° elbow angle.

Saturday.name: Low-Incline Dumbbell Flye
Saturday.notes: Focus on feeling a deep stretch in your pecs at the bottom of each rep. Keep the dumbbells in the bottom ~3/4 of the range of motion as there will be no tension on the pecs at the top. We are including these because the dumbbells' resistance profile really accentuates the stretch.

Saturday.name: Katana Triceps Extension
Saturday.notes: Flare your elbows out at about 45° and keep your elbows locked in place as you complete the extensions.

Saturday.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
Saturday.notes: Don't just bend at your hips, use your abs to lower yourself down under control and pull yourself back up. If you don't have the core strength to get all the way extended at the bottom, try to progressively increase the ROM week to week.

Sunday.name: Lying Leg Curl
Sunday.notes: Set the machine so that you get the biggest stretch possible at the bottom. Prevent your butt from popping up as you curl.

Sunday.name: Leg Press
Sunday.notes: Feet lower on the platform for more quad focus. Get as deep as you can without excessive back rounding. Control the negative and do a slight pause at the bottom of each rep. Try to add a little weight each week at the same rep count.

Sunday.name: Smith Machine Lunge
Sunday.notes: 2 sets each leg. Minimize contribution from the back leg. Mind-muscle connection with your glutes here!

Sunday.name: A1: Machine Hip Adduction
Sunday.notes: Mind-muscle connection with your inner thighs. These are great for adding thigh mass from the front! Push them hard!

Sunday.name: A2: Sissy Squat
Sunday.notes: Allow yourself to come up onto your toes and push your knees forward past your toes. This is safe for the knees. If you feel knee pain doing them, though, feel free to go with a substitution. They may feel awkward at first, but they're really underrated for the quads! Don't give up on them too quickly.

Sunday.name: Standing Calf Raise
Sunday.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Weak Point Exercise 1
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Weak Point Exercise 2 (optional)
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: If your weak point is feeling recovered (not sore or fatigued) then feel free to hit Exercise 2. If your weak point is feeling tired or sore, do not perform the second weak point exercise this week.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Cable Skull Crusher
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Allow the cable to move back behind your head for maximum stretch on the triceps. Reach full extension with your elbows still around eye level at the top.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Kneeling Overhead Cable Curl
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Kneel down, stick your arm straight out to the side and do cable curls. You should feel a tight squeeze at the top of each rep on these.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Triceps Diverging Pressdown (Long Rope or 2 Ropes)
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Use two long ropes or one long rope. Lean slightly forward, flare your elbows slightly out and keep your arms back in line with your torso. Then do triceps pressdowns, getting a full, big squeeze at the bottom.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Incline DB Stretch-Curl
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Keep your upper back planted against the bench, rotate your arms outward until you feel a massive stretch in your biceps. Go light on these and instead focus on feeling your biceps pull and squeeze.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Cable Crunch
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.

Sunday.name: A1: Machine Hip Adduction

Sunday.name: A2: Sissy Squat

Tuesday.name: Cuffed Behind-The-Back Lateral Raise

Wednesday.name: Seated Leg Curl

Wednesday.name: Machine Hip Adduction
Wednesday.notes: Mind-muscle connection with your inner thighs. These are great for adding thigh mass from the front! Push them hard!

Wednesday.name: Smith Machine Squat

Wednesday.name: Leg Extension
Wednesday.notes: Set the seat back as far as it will go while still feeling comfortable. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat. Use a 2-3 second negative. Feel your quads pulling apart on the negative.

Wednesday.name: DB Calf Jumps
Wednesday.notes: Hold a dumbbell and perform a jumping motion without actually leaving the floor, using a slight knee bend, but mostly relying on your calves/ankles to drive the "jump". I believe I built a lot of calf mass by doing jump rope; these are meant to provide a similar stimulus, but with more tension.

Thursday.name: Weak Point Exercise 1
Thursday.notes: Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

Thursday.name: Weak Point Exercise 2 (optional)
Thursday.notes: If your weak point is feeling recovered (not sore or fatigued) then feel free to hit Exercise 2. If your weak point is feeling tired or sore, do not perform the second weak point exercise this week.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

Thursday.name: Slow-Eccentric EZ-Bar Skull Crusher
Thursday.notes: Use a 3-4 second negative. Arc the EZ-bar slightly back behind your head. When you extend, keep the bar back behind your eye line. Use the inside (closer) grip option and allow the elbows to flare to a degree that feels comfortable.

Thursday.name: Slow-Eccentric Bayesian Curl
Thursday.notes: Use a 3-4 second negative and a slight pause at the bottom of each rep to emphasize stretching your biceps.

Thursday.name: Triceps Diverging Pressdown (Long Rope or 2 Ropes)
Thursday.notes: Use two long ropes or one long rope. Lean slightly forward, flare your elbows slightly out and keep your arms back in line with your torso. Then do triceps pressdowns, getting a full, big squeeze at the bottom.

Thursday.name: Reverse-Grip Cable Curl
Thursday.notes: Grab a cable bar with your palms facing down and perform curls. These will work the back of your forearm, brachialis and biceps!

Thursday.name: Roman Chair Leg Raise
Thursday.notes: Allow your lower back to round as you curl your legs up. 10-20 reps is a broad range on purpose: just go until you hit RPE 9-10 (0-1 reps shy of failure) with controlled form.

Friday.name: Dual-Handle Lat Pulldown (Mid-back + Lats)
Friday.notes: Lean back by ~15° and drive your elbows down as you squeeze your shoulder blades together. This should feel like a mix of lats and mid-traps.

Friday.name: Arms-Extended 45° Hyperextension
Friday.notes: Keep your arms extended out at 45° as you do back extensions. You should feel a crazy burn in your mid-back and lower back.

Friday.name: Chest-Supported Machine Row
Friday.notes: Flare elbows out at roughly 45° and squeeze your shoulder blades together hard at the top of each rep.

Friday.name: Concentration Cable Curl
Friday.notes: Place your working elbow against your knee and perform strict form curls.

Friday.name: Rear Delt 45° Cable Flye
Friday.notes: Pull with one arm at a time, bracing with your non-working hand against the machine. Try to align your arm and the cable in a straight line at the bottom of the flye.

Saturday.name: Seated DB Shoulder Press
Saturday.notes: Slightly rotate the dumbbells in on the negative and flare your elbows out as you push.

Saturday.name: Cross-Body Cable Y-Raise
Saturday.notes: Think about "drawing a sword" as you do the positive. Sweep your arm up, out and across your body. It may take a few weeks to get used to these if you haven't done them before, but once they click, they really click.

Saturday.name: Decline Machine Chest Press
Saturday.notes: Feel your pecs stretching apart on the negative. Mind-muscle connection with lower pecs.

Saturday.name: Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (Bar)
Saturday.notes: Feel a nasty stretch on the triceps throughout the entire negative. Pause for 1 second in the stretch part of each rep.

Saturday.name: Stomach Vacuums
Saturday.notes: Suck your stomach in and hold it for 10-15 seconds, repeat 2x.

Saturday.name: Super-ROM DB Lateral Raise
Saturday.notes: Perform lateral raises as normal, except going until your hands are up overhead. As you break parallel, you will use more upper traps to move the weight. Feel free to squeeze your upper traps at the top. If you feel shoulder pain when going all the way up, try pointing your thumb up or simply stop at parallel and do normal lateral raises.

Sunday.name: Lying Leg Curl
Sunday.notes: Set the machine so that you get the biggest stretch possible at the bottom. Prevent your butt from popping up as you curl.

Sunday.name: Smith Machine Reverse Lunge
Sunday.notes: Set one leg back on the negative and then drive the weight up using your front leg. Try to minimize assistance from your back leg.

Sunday.name: Leg Extension
Sunday.notes: Set the seat back as far as it will go. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat. Use a 2-3 second negative. Feel your quads pulling apart on the negative.

Sunday.name: A1: Machine Hip Adduction
Sunday.notes: Mind-muscle connection with your inner thighs. These are great for adding thigh mass from the front! Push them hard!

Sunday.name: A2: Machine Hip Abduction
Sunday.notes: If possible, use pads to increase the range of motion on the machine. Lean forward and grab onto the machine rails to stretch the glutes further.

Sunday.name: Standing Calf Raise
Sunday.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Weak Point Exercise 1
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Weak Point Exercise 2 (optional)
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: If your weak point is feeling recovered (not sore or fatigued) then feel free to hit Exercise 2. If your weak point is feeling tired or sore, do not perform the second weak point exercise this week.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Triceps Pressdown (Bar)
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: These are meant to be fairly heavy, which is why we're using a bar instead of a rope. Aim to add some weight week to week. Always keep the form tight as you overload the triceps.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Fat-Grip DB Curl
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Squeeze the dumbbell hard in the middle of the handle as you curl. Using liquid chalk on these will prevent your grip from slipping, keeping your hand in the middle of the handle throughout the set (as opposed to resting against the head of the dumbbell).

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Cable Triceps Kickback
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: There are two ways you can do this: upright or bent over. Choose the one that feels more comfortable for you. The main thing is that when you're in the full squeeze, your shoulder should be positioned back behind your torso.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Medicine Ball Russian Twists
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Keep the ball held out far from your body on the sides and control the reps, don't just rush through them to get the set done.

Monday.name: Lat-Focused Cable Row
Monday.notes: Keep your torso locked in a fixed position (don't lean forward on the negative). Drive your elbows down and back to engage the lats. Keep your elbows tucked in to your sides.

Monday.name: Paused Barbell RDL
Monday.notes: The RPE is intentionally low here because these will cause a lot of muscle damage. Don't be tempted to go too heavy. 1 second pause at the bottom of each rep. To keep tension on the hamstrings, stop about 75% of the way to full lockout on each rep (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the range of motion).

Monday.name: Chest-Supported T-Bar Row + Kelso Shrug
Monday.notes: Do 8-10 reps as a normal T-Bar row, driving your elbows back at roughly 45° and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Without resting, do another 4-6 reps as Kelso Shrugs (just squeeze your shoulder blades together without rowing all the way back with your arms).
substitutions: Incline Chest-Supported DB Row + Kelso Shrug

Monday.name: 1-Arm Lat Pull-In
Monday.notes: Pull the cable in from the side. Keep a mind-muscle connection with your lats and try to prevent your biceps from taking over. Palpate (feel) your lats with your other hand if that helps you connect with them better.

Monday.name: N1-Style Short-Head Curl
Monday.notes: Brace against your knee with your non-working hand and curl across your body, toward your opposite shoulder.

Monday.name: Reverse Pec Deck (w/ Integrated Partials)
Monday.notes: Sweep the weight out instead of pulling the weight back. Mind-muscle connection with rear delts. On all sets, alternate full-ROM reps and half-ROM reps (i.e. do 1 rep with full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM (in the stretched/bottom half), then 1 rep full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM). Repeat until you've reached the target reps and an RPE of 9-10.
substitutions: Bent-Over Reverse DB Flye (w/ Integrated Partials)

Tuesday.name: Bent-Over Cable Pec Flye (w/ Integrated Partials)

Monday.name: 1-Arm Lat Pull-In
Monday.notes: Pull the cable in from the side. Keep a mind-muscle connection with your lats and try to prevent your biceps from taking over. Palpate (feel) your lats with your other hand if that helps you connect with them better.

Monday.name: N1-Style Short-Head Curl
Monday.notes: Brace against your knee with your non-working hand and curl across your body, toward your opposite shoulder.

Monday.name: Reverse Pec Deck (w/ Integrated Partials)
Monday.notes: Sweep the weight out instead of pulling the weight back. Mind-muscle connection with rear delts. On all sets, alternate full-ROM reps and half-ROM reps (i.e. do 1 rep with full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM (in the stretched/bottom half), then 1 rep full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM). Repeat until you've reached the target reps and an RPE of 9-10.
substitutions: Bent-Over Reverse DB Flye (w/ Integrated Partials)

Tuesday.name: Cuffed Behind-The-Back Lateral Raise
Tuesday.notes: Raise the cables up in a "Y" motion. Really try to connect with the middle delt fibers as you sweep the weight up and out.

Tuesday.name: Low Incline DB Press
Tuesday.notes: Set the bench at a ~15° incline. Do a slight elbow tuck on the negative and then flare as you push (assuming this doesn't bother your shoulders). Nice, smooth reps here. No pausing at the top or bottom: constant tension on the pecs!

Tuesday.name: Dual-Cable Triceps Press
Tuesday.notes: Hold the cables without a handle and get them into position just above your shoulders (around chin level). Press the weight forward (straight out in front of you), not up overhead like in a standard overhead triceps extension.

Tuesday.name: Bent-Over Cable Pec Flye (w/ Integrated Partials)
Tuesday.notes: Lean forward until your torso is parallel with the floor, flye straight out and down toward the floor. Stretch and squeeze the pecs! Stay locked in.

Tuesday.name: Deficit Pushup
Tuesday.notes: Slow negative with a deep stretch at the bottom of each rep before exploding back up on the positive.

Tuesday.name: Cable Crunch
Tuesday.notes: Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.

Wednesday.name: Seated Leg Curl
Wednesday.notes: Lean forward over the machine to get a maximum stretch in your hamstrings.

Wednesday.name: Machine Hip Adduction
Wednesday.notes: Mind-muscle connection with your inner thighs. These are great for adding thigh mass from the front! Push them hard!

Wednesday.name: Smith Machine Squat
Wednesday.notes: We're using a reverse pyramid on this exercise. Warm-up as usual to your first working set for 4 reps. This first set will be your heaviest set. Then for set 2, drop the weight back ~10-15% and do 6 reps. Then for set 3, drop the weight back another 10-15% and do 8 reps.

Wednesday.name: Leg Extension
Wednesday.notes: Set the seat back as far as it will go while still feeling comfortable. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat. Use a 2-3 second negative. Feel your quads pulling apart on the negative.

Wednesday.name: DB Calf Jumps
Wednesday.notes: Hold a dumbbell and perform a jumping motion without actually leaving the floor, using a slight knee bend, but mostly relying on your calves/ankles to drive the "jump". I believe I built a lot of calf mass by doing jump rope; these are meant to provide a similar stimulus, but with more tension.

Thursday.name: Weak Point Exercise 1
Thursday.notes: Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

Thursday.name: Weak Point Exercise 2 (optional)
Thursday.notes: If your weak point is feeling recovered (not sore or fatigued) then feel free to hit Exercise 2. If your weak point is feeling tired or sore, do not perform the second weak point exercise this week.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

Thursday.name: Slow-Eccentric EZ-Bar Skull Crusher
Thursday.notes: Use a 3-4 second negative. Arc the EZ-bar slightly back behind your head. When you extend, keep the bar back behind your eye line. Use the inside (closer) grip option and allow the elbows to flare to a degree that feels comfortable.

Thursday.name: Slow-Eccentric Bayesian Curl
Thursday.notes: Use a 3-4 second negative and a slight pause at the bottom of each rep to emphasize stretching your biceps.

Thursday.name: Triceps Diverging Pressdown (Long Rope or 2 Ropes)
Thursday.notes: Use two long ropes or one long rope. Lean slightly forward, flare your elbows slightly out and keep your arms back in line with your torso. Then do triceps pressdowns, getting a full, big squeeze at the bottom.

Thursday.name: Reverse-Grip Cable Curl
Thursday.notes: Grab a cable bar with your palms facing down and perform curls. These will work the back of your forearm, brachialis and biceps!

Thursday.name: Roman Chair Leg Raise
Thursday.notes: Allow your lower back to round as you curl your legs up. 10-20 reps is a broad range on purpose: just go until you hit RPE 9-10 (0-1 reps shy of failure) with controlled form.

Friday.name: Dual-Handle Lat Pulldown (Mid-back + Lats)
Friday.notes: Lean back by ~15° and drive your elbows down as you squeeze your shoulder blades together. This should feel like a mix of lats and mid-traps.

Friday.name: Arms-Extended 45° Hyperextension
Friday.notes: Keep your arms extended out at 45° as you do back extensions. You should feel a crazy burn in your mid-back and lower back.

Friday.name: Chest-Supported Machine Row
Friday.notes: Flare elbows out at roughly 45° and squeeze your shoulder blades together hard at the top of each rep.

Friday.name: Concentration Cable Curl
Friday.notes: Place your working elbow against your knee and perform strict form curls.

Friday.name: Rear Delt 45° Cable Flye
Friday.notes: Pull with one arm at a time, bracing with your non-working hand against the machine. Try to align your arm and the cable in a straight line at the bottom of the flye.

Saturday.name: Seated DB Shoulder Press
Saturday.notes: Slightly rotate the dumbbells in on the negative and flare your elbows out as you push.

Saturday.name: Cross-Body Cable Y-Raise
Saturday.notes: Think about "drawing a sword" as you do the positive. Sweep your arm up, out and across your body. It may take a few weeks to get used to these if you haven't done them before, but once they click, they really click.

Saturday.name: Decline Machine Chest Press
Saturday.notes: Feel your pecs stretching apart on the negative. Mind-muscle connection with lower pecs.

Saturday.name: Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (Bar)
Saturday.notes: Feel a nasty stretch on the triceps throughout the entire negative. Pause for 1 second in the stretch part of each rep.

Saturday.name: Stomach Vacuums
Saturday.notes: Suck your stomach in and hold it for 10-15 seconds, repeat 2x.

Saturday.name: Super-ROM DB Lateral Raise
Saturday.notes: Perform lateral raises as normal, except going until your hands are up overhead. As you break parallel, you will use more upper traps to move the weight. Feel free to squeeze your upper traps at the top. If you feel shoulder pain when going all the way up, try pointing your thumb up or simply stop at parallel and do normal lateral raises.

Sunday.name: Lying Leg Curl
Sunday.notes: Set the machine so that you get the biggest stretch possible at the bottom. Prevent your butt from popping up as you curl.

Sunday.name: Smith Machine Reverse Lunge
Sunday.notes: Set one leg back on the negative and then drive the weight up using your front leg. Try to minimize assistance from your back leg.

Sunday.name: Leg Extension
Sunday.notes: Set the seat back as far as it will go. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat. Use a 2-3 second negative. Feel your quads pulling apart on the negative.

Sunday.name: A1: Machine Hip Adduction
Sunday.notes: Mind-muscle connection with your inner thighs. These are great for adding thigh mass from the front! Push them hard!

Sunday.name: A2: Machine Hip Abduction
Sunday.notes: If possible, use pads to increase the range of motion on the machine. Lean forward and grab onto the machine rails to stretch the glutes further.

Sunday.name: Standing Calf Raise
Sunday.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Weak Point Exercise 1
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Weak Point Exercise 2 (optional)
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: If your weak point is feeling recovered (not sore or fatigued) then feel free to hit Exercise 2. If your weak point is feeling tired or sore, do not perform the second weak point exercise this week.
substitutions: See The Weak Point Table for sub options

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Triceps Pressdown (Bar)
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: These are meant to be fairly heavy, which is why we're using a bar instead of a rope. Aim to add some weight week to week. Always keep the form tight as you overload the triceps.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Fat-Grip DB Curl
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Squeeze the dumbbell hard in the middle of the handle as you curl. Using liquid chalk on these will prevent your grip from slipping, keeping your hand in the middle of the handle throughout the set (as opposed to resting against the head of the dumbbell).

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Cable Triceps Kickback
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: There are two ways you can do this: upright or bent over. Choose the one that feels more comfortable for you. The main thing is that when you're in the full squeeze, your shoulder should be positioned back behind your torso.

ArmsWeakPoints2.name: Medicine Ball Russian Twists
ArmsWeakPoints2.notes: Keep the ball held out far from your body on the sides and control the reps, don't just rush through them to get the set done.

Monday.name: Lat-Focused Cable Row
Monday.notes: Keep your torso locked in a fixed position (don't lean forward on the negative). Drive your elbows down and back to engage the lats. Keep your elbows tucked in to your sides.

Monday.name: Paused Barbell RDL
Monday.notes: The RPE is intentionally low here because these will cause a lot of muscle damage. Don't be tempted to go too heavy. 1 second pause at the bottom of each rep. To keep tension on the hamstrings, stop about 75% of the way to full lockout on each rep (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the range of motion).
